codeReview.summary: The homepage carousel layout implementation is solid and covers all 5 required sections (Popular Events, Football Matches, Live Concerts, Basketball Games, Team Merchandise) using the HorizontalCarousel component with correct card types and See All links. A new /products listing page was created for the Team Merchandise See All target. Loading skeletons are shown during data fetch. The BannerCarousel is preserved at the top. Tests cover all major scenarios. Two non-blocking issues found: (1) the Popular Events 'See All' link points to /football rather than a general events page — this is semantically imprecise for a mixed-type carousel, but acceptable since no /events listing page exists, (2) fetch calls don't check response.ok before parsing JSON, which could cause silent failures on API errors.

issues.description: Popular Events 'See All' link points to /football, but this carousel shows a mix of all event types (football, concert, basketball). This is semantically misleading — users clicking 'See All' for popular events would expect to see all popular events, not just football. No general /events listing page exists, so /football was used as a fallback. Consider creating an /events page or removing the See All link for this mixed section.

Fetch calls do not check response.ok before calling .json(). If any API endpoint returns a non-2xx status (e.g., 500 error), the code will attempt to parse the error response JSON and set it as state data, potentially causing rendering errors with incorrect data shapes. Should add response.ok checks and throw on failure for each of the 5 fetched endpoints.

Extensive use of TypeScript non-null assertions (!) on optional Event fields (team1, team2, team1Flag, team2Flag, artistName, artistImageUrl). While these are guarded by event.type-based branching, if the API returns malformed data (e.g., a football event with null team1), this would crash at runtime. The type branching makes this low risk but not zero risk.
See All link test verifies /football, /concert, and /basketball links but does not assert the /products link for Team Merchandise. This is a minor test coverage gap.

New /products listing page was created but has no dedicated test file. The homepage tests verify carousels but don't test the products listing page rendering, empty state, or loading skeleton behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The homepage page.tsx does not check response.ok on fetch responses before parsing JSON. Other pages in the codebase (e.g., category pages) also follow this pattern of not checking response.ok. This is a project-wide convention gap — AGENTS.md doesn't document a standard pattern for client-side fetch error handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/page.tsx lines 63-73: five fetch().json() calls with no response.ok checks. Same pattern in app/football/page.tsx, app/concert/page.tsx, app/basketball/page.tsx.

sharedStateObservations.observation: HorizontalCarousel.tsx (from prior feature) has a duplicate CSS class 'flex items-center' on line 44 of the See All link element. This is harmless but indicates no Tailwind class deduplication linting is enforced.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: components/ui/HorizontalCarousel.tsx:44 — className contains 'flex items-center' twice in the same string.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The platform-worker skill prescribes TDD ('Write tests first'), but the worker's transcript skeleton and handoff suggest implementation-then-testing approach. The worker's skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations noted, despite not strictly following TDD. The skill's TDD requirement may be aspirational rather than enforced — consider clarifying in the skill whether strict TDD is required or test-alongside-implementation is acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 2 says 'Write tests first (TDD): Write failing tests for the feature before implementing.' Handoff and transcript show implementation happened before test creation. skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, deviations: [].

summary: PASS. The homepage carousel layout feature is well-implemented with all 5 required sections, correct card types, See All navigation, loading skeletons, and preserved BannerCarousel. A new /products listing page was created for the Team Merchandise See All link. 7 comprehensive tests added. All 405 tests pass, typecheck and lint clean. Non-blocking issues: Popular Events See All links to /football (semantically imprecise for mixed carousel), no response.ok checks on fetch calls, and minor test coverage gaps (no /products link assertion, no dedicated products page test). Overall code quality is good, follows project conventions (TypeScript strict, Tailwind, Next.js App Router, existing card components), and the interactive browser verification confirmed correct mobile rendering.
